channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: US pauses arms sales to Taiwan over Iran war
item.description: The stalled $19.6 billion weapons purchase by Taiwan has been awaiting sign-off from US President Donald Trump.

media:group.media:description: Taiwan says it has not been told about a change to its weapons deal with the United States.

item.title: Labor leaves door open to an additional trust tax exemption
item.description: The federal government has left the door open to further changes after coming under pressure for including future discretionary testamentary trusts in a new tax announced in the budget.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Labor is also working on carve-outs for startup businesses that could be impacted.

item.title: Live: Battered Bombers fighting hard despite losing three to injury, Freo face Saints
item.description: Richmond and Essendon each look to add to their lone wins of the season when they clash in Dreamtime at the 'G, while Fremantle hosts St Kilda eyeing a 10th straight win. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Henry Hanson and Dean Bilton

media:group.media:description: McGrath was one of three Essendon players to be ruled out of the game in the first half at the MCG.

item.title: Live: The Bulldogs snap horror losing streak to come from behind to beat the Storm
item.description: Matt Burton has inspired inspired the Canterbury Bankstown Bulldogs to a 30-20 come-from-behind victory against the Melbourne Storm, snapping their five-game losing streak. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Sitili Tupouniua crashed over soon after half time.

item.title: Pacific leaders warned of 'prolonged' fuel crisis as new plan endorsed
item.description: A taskforce will see leaders get a better idea of threats to energy deliveries, while Australia and New Zealand provide expert analysis on future supply.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Dziedzic

media:group.media:description: The cost of fuel has become a serious issue in many Pacific countries since the Iran war began.

item.title: Mental health evidence delays sentencing of Molly Ticehurst's murderer
item.description: The defence team for Daniel Billings has submitted evidence from a forensic psychiatrist that found he was in an "abnormal state of mind" when he killed his former partner.
item.dc:creator: Lani Oataway

media:group.media:description: Molly Ticehurst was found dead in her Forbes home in 2024.

item.title: Melbourne man found guilty of keeping woman as a slave
item.description: The court heard the man had beaten the woman and deprived her of food while forcing her into unpaid labour at his home.
item.dc:creator: Kristian Silva

media:group.media:description: Angie Yeh Liaw and Chee Kit (Max) Chong leave the County Court in March.

item.title: A woman has died after being hit by a police car. What happens now?
item.description: A criminology expert and former police officer provides insight into what the investigation into Boksoon Kwon's death will involve.
item.dc:creator: Rosanne Maloney

media:group.media:description: A shoe left on the road after the incident was later placed into a police evidence bag.

item.title: IS-linked families arrive in Damascus ahead of journey to Australia
item.description: After leaving the al-Roj refugee camp on Thursday, a group of seven Australian women and 14 children has arrived in the Syrian capital, Damascus.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Doran and Fiona Willan

media:group.media:description: A group of Australians has left the al-Roj refugee camp in Syria.

item.title: 'No sign of danger': Friend details horror of fatal shark attack
item.description: The best friend of a Perth father killed by a shark off Rottnest Island says there was "absolutely no warning" before the attack.
item.dc:creator: Michael Tetlow

media:group.media:description: Steven Mattaboni leaves behind his two daughters and wife Shirene.

item.title: Rex Airlines announces service cuts in Victoria and Tasmania
item.description: The embattled carrier has announced it is ceasing some services and reducing others.
item.dc:creator: Wade Stephens

media:group.media:description: Regional Express has announced a service restructure in response to fuel price hikes.

item.title: Boat driver jailed after running over swimmer who suffered 'horrific injuries'
item.description: A woman who was likely more than three times the legal blood alcohol limit when she ran over a swimmer while driving a boat has been jailed for more than two years.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: Amy Lohf, seen in mobile phone vision in the moments before the incident.

item.title: 'Pink lady' denies drafting her own job description before council interview
item.description: The commission was shown a text to the former chief executive of Parramatta Council from Roxanne Thornton featuring a handwritten note detailing her salary and entitlements.
item.dc:creator: Anton Rose, Nabil Al-Nashar, and Miriah Davis

media:group.media:description: Roxanne Thornton was grilled on Friday over claims of misconduct.

item.title: Solution arrives for crash-prone 'Russian roulette' intersection
item.description: After almost a decade of campaigning, residents welcome safety improvements at a notorious stretch of the Princes Highway considered the most crash-prone turn-off in southern New South Wales.
item.dc:creator: Charles Rushforth

media:group.media:description: The intersection of Jervis Bay Road and the Princes Highway is the busiest road from Nowra to the Victorian border.

item.title: Scam operations reported to be relocating across borders after crackdowns
item.description: A growing number of scam operations are cropping up in Sri Lanka amid sweeping crackdowns in hotspot countries elsewhere in the region.

media:group.media:description: Police busted an alleged scam operation staffed by Chinese nationals in Colombo earlier this month.

item.title: Location of first country Victorian police academy announced
item.description: Victoria Police announces plans to trial its first regional police training academy in Mildura next year before moving the program to other regional centres in the following years.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Buchecker and Grace Marshall

media:group.media:description: The new academy in Mildura will train 100 recruits each year.

item.title: The Olympic past behind Tolu Koula's rise to State of Origin
item.description: Manly speedster Tolu Koula is set to bring his pace to the Origin arena at the same stadium where his parents represented Tonga in the Sydney Olympics.

media:group.media:description: Tolu Koula will bring his electrifying speed to the Origin arena.

item.title: Albury Wodonga Health executive exodus continues as board chair departs
item.description: The exodus of executives is continuing at embattled Albury Wodonga Health, with the board chair announcing he will not be seeking reappointment when his term ends.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Mangelsdorf

media:group.media:description: Jonathan Green will not seek reappointment when his term ends next month.

item.title: Woman who died in house fire 'rejected' accused murderer, court hears
item.description: The prosecution alleges the 62-year-old farmstay guest was "unable to cope" after the deceased declined to enter into a romantic relationship with him.
item.dc:creator: Anna McGuinness

media:group.media:description: Maree Vermont was allegedly murdered at her home in Goldie, Victoria.

item.title: Investigation into King Charles's brother, Andrew, will be lengthy, UK police say
item.description: The British police investigation into Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor will be long and complex, senior officers say, after his arrest earlier this year on suspicion of misconduct in public office, an offence that can include sexual impropriety.

media:group.media:description: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor was stripped of his royal titles in October.

item.title: Pair jailed for manslaughter of man shot in head
item.description: Two people have been jailed for their role in the shooting death of 25-year-old Thor Morgan, who was attacked in a suburban Toowoomba street in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Grace Nakamura

media:group.media:description: Thor Morgan died in May, two months after being shot in the head.

item.title: Stephen Colbert wraps up Late Show with Paul McCartney and a fake pope
item.description: While Colbert's CBS network came in for plenty of jibes over the course of the episode, it was a largely apolitical affair, with attacks on US President Donald Trump ditched in favour of joy, jokes and songs.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Thorpe

media:group.media:description: Sir Paul McCartney was the final guest on the 33-year-old Late Show franchise.

item.title: China and Russia block conservation bid for endangered emperor penguin
item.description: The annual meeting of Antarctic Treaty nations has failed to reach consensus to designate the endangered emperor penguin as a "Specially Protected Species".
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: Conservation group, WWF said the Emperor penguin needed urgent protection

item.title: 'How are you, mate?' Bodycam footage shown of police officer's fatal shooting
item.description: A court hears that some of the last words uttered by a policeman was to ask the man who fatally shot him, "how are you, mate?"
item.dc:creator: Sandy Powell

media:group.media:description: Keith Smith and Leigh Sushames composite image.

item.title: 'Arc de Trump' is one step closer to being built. Here's why it's controversial
item.description: The Arc de Triomphe-inspired arch in Washington DC is meant to commemorate the US's 250th anniversary but its location is causing controversy.
item.dc:creator: Hanan Dervisevic

media:group.media:description: An artist's impression of the proposed "Arc de Trump". Remember the lions have now been removed.
